executor_evidence: T01 created comprehensive Phase 3 helper contract test suite covering: mailbox path helpers, ownership path helpers, mailbox message schema (all 8 message_type values, all 5 status values, all 11 canonical fields including task_id), ownership registry schema (work_id, entries), ownership entry schema (all 10 canonical fields, all 5 modes, all 4 statuses, all 6 operations), target_type 'path' only with glob rejection, ownership mode/operation/actor invariant enforcement (read_only, exclusive_write, shared_append, orchestrator_owned, sequenced_write with serial-batching contract assertion), shared ownership representation with multiple shared_append entries, append-only bootstrap creation representation, orchestrator ownership representation, path constraints (no absolute paths, no .., no empty segments, no drive prefixes), OwnershipEvidenceSchema (all canonical fields, attribution methods, coverage statuses), omitted-evidence failure detection helper, global ownership path-coverage helper, SecurityTriggerCategorySchema (all 9 categories), SecurityTriggerEvidenceSchema (all 3 decisions), full VerificationRecordSchema passthrough with Phase 3 evidence validation, and source-text purity assertion. Spec-review fix: added missing task_id canonical field rejection test for mailbox messages. Spec-review fix: added explicit sequenced_write serial-batching contract/expectation test documenting that multiple sequenced_write entries for the same target require serial batch sequencing and must not be scheduled in the same parallel batch. RED confirmed: module does not exist.

diagnostics_status.summary: Test file imports non-existent module; import-time failure is expected RED state. No LSP typecheck applicable until T02 creates the helper module.

notes: T01 creates the helper contract test file and its own verification record.
T01 appends to mailbox.jsonl and ledger.jsonl using shared_append ownership.
Pre-existing artifacts (spec.md, plan.md, ownership.json, state.json, T00.json) are read-only or owned by other tasks and were not modified by T01.

T01 creates test fixtures only. No code execution, filesystem mutation logic, shell commands, authentication, secrets handling, git operations, network calls, dependency changes, sandbox behavior, or untrusted-input handling is introduced. Security triggers do not apply.
